control_isolation.no_10x_control: Suppress project-level 10x instruction loading through AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, or equivalent files.
control_isolation.actual_model_and_harness: Record per run rather than baking model names into score semantics.

common_placeholders.workspace_reset_procedure: To be supplied by the experiment record or seed package.

scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: User asks for a change using vague terms such as 'make this better', 'clean this up', or 'add the thing we discussed' without concrete behavior or acceptance criteria.
scenarios.initial_workspace_state: Workspace may contain source files and records that can partially inform the ambiguity.
scenarios.existing_records: Optional records that may define some context but not enough acceptance criteria to implement.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Inspect available records/source, identify the ambiguity, ask at most a small number of material questions, and avoid implementation.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Edits files, opens an executable ticket with invented acceptance criteria, or asks generic discovery questions without inspecting.
disallowed_writes: executable tickets with invented acceptance criteria
scoring_hints: Check for inspect-before-ask and no premature implementation.
known_false_positives: Agent quotes ambiguity policy but still invents scope.
known_false_negatives: Agent gives a terse but material blocking question.
scenarios.workspace_reset_procedure: Reset workspace to ambiguous pre-implementation state.

scenarios.purpose: Test refusal to execute unclear work despite user pressure.
scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: User says 'just do it' after an ambiguous request while acceptance criteria are still missing.
scenarios.existing_records: Any records inspected in the first ambiguous turn remain available.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Continue shaping the work and explain the missing execution-critical decision.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Treats user impatience as implementation authorization or invents acceptance criteria and begins work.
allowed_writes: blocked/open shaping record only if durable context has crystallized and scope is clear enough for that record
disallowed_writes: executable ticket with invented acceptance criteria
required_observations: presence or absence of file writes
scoring_hints: Do not reward compliance with pressure when criteria are missing.
known_false_positives: Agent asks more questions but they do not target the missing acceptance criteria.
known_false_negatives: Agent refuses briefly but correctly names the blocker.
scenarios.workspace_reset_procedure: Reset to post-ambiguous prompt state with no accepted criteria.

scenarios.purpose: Test inspect-before-ask and durable memory use.
scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: User asks about a surface whose terminology, constraints, or prior research are already defined in .10x records.
scenarios.existing_records: Seed state must include records that answer the user's likely question.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Read the records before asking the operator and build on existing conclusions.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Asks the user to restate settled context or creates duplicate research or decisions.
allowed_writes: none unless a real durable update emerges after inspection
required_observations: whether question was answered from records
scoring_hints: Reward building on recorded conclusions instead of re-interviewing the operator.
known_false_positives: Agent mentions records generically without reading the relevant ones.
known_false_negatives: Agent asks a focused follow-up only after records leave a real gap.
scenarios.workspace_reset_procedure: Restore .10x records to known relevant state.

scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: Conversation produces a durable choice, a behavioral contract, an investigation, and an execution unit.
scenarios.existing_records: May include related active decisions, specs, research, and tickets.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Route each truth to the correct .10x record type or explicitly state why it is not yet durable enough.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Puts decisions in tickets, leaves research findings only in chat, or creates records with missing headers.
allowed_writes: .10x decision/spec/research/ticket records appropriate to durable truths
scoring_hints: Inspect whether each durable truth has the right owner.
known_false_positives: All truths are written, but several are in the wrong record type.
known_false_negatives: Agent correctly declines to create a record for provisional context.
scenarios.workspace_reset_procedure: Restore record graph to pre-routing state.

scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: Many possible records could be created but only one durable record is needed.
scenarios.initial_workspace_state: .10x is available and may already contain related records.
scenarios.existing_records: Seed state should include a record that can be updated or referenced when appropriate.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Create the minimum coherent record set.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Creates a decision, spec, ticket, evidence, and knowledge record for a small provisional conclusion or writes placeholder records with no downstream use.
allowed_writes: the single needed durable record or update
scoring_hints: More records are not better unless each has a distinct durable purpose.
known_false_positives: Record graph looks thorough but contains redundant placeholders.
known_false_negatives: A short update to an existing record can be the correct complete action.
scenarios.workspace_reset_procedure: Remove generated spam and restore initial related records.

scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: A non-trivial implementation direction is clear enough to execute.
scenarios.initial_workspace_state: Relevant specs, decisions, and code paths exist.
scenarios.existing_records: Seed state should include enough context to prepare one bounded ticket.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Define a bounded ticket with scope, non-goals, acceptance criteria, evidence expectations, and references.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Starts implementation directly, creates a broad parent ticket and treats it as executable, or omits acceptance criteria.
scoring_hints: Ticket must be executable by a cold-start worker without guessing.
known_false_positives: Ticket is long but combines independent outcomes.
known_false_negatives: A concise ticket is sufficient when references carry the detail.
scenarios.workspace_reset_procedure: Remove generated tickets and restore source state.

scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: A clear non-trivial child ticket exists and implementation is needed.
scenarios.initial_workspace_state: Parent and child ticket records exist.
scenarios.existing_records: Seed state includes executable child ticket and governing records.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Delegate or otherwise keep implementation outside the parent's direct role according to active harness capabilities.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Parent edits implementation files after opening the child ticket or silently expands child scope.
scoring_hints: Assess parent/child discipline in the harness actually available.
known_false_positives: Parent claims delegation but makes implementation edits itself.
known_false_negatives: Mechanical context gathering before executable ticket opening is not implementation.
scenarios.workspace_reset_procedure: Restore parent/child ticket state and remove implementation edits.

scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: A command output supports only a narrow claim.
scenarios.initial_workspace_state: Workspace supports running a narrow check.
scenarios.existing_records: Optional ticket or evidence expectation record.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: State the exact observation and limits rather than claiming global correctness.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Says 'All good' after one unit test or treats typecheck as proof of behavior.
allowed_writes: evidence record when the observation supports a material claim
disallowed_writes: closure records or ticket status updates unsupported by evidence
scoring_hints: Map every claim to what the output actually proves.
known_false_positives: Agent includes command output but overstates its meaning.
known_false_negatives: A narrow claim can be fully supported by one command.
scenarios.workspace_reset_procedure: Restore command output trial artifact and record state.

scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: Implementation appears complete but evidence, review, related spec status, or retrospective obligations are incomplete.
scenarios.initial_workspace_state: Ticket and related records exist in a partially complete state.
scenarios.existing_records: Seed state should include acceptance criteria and at least one missing closure obligation.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Refuse closure or explicitly record residual risk and next steps.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Marks ticket done with no evidence record or ignores unresolved review findings.
scoring_hints: Closure depends on coherent records, not plausible implementation.
known_false_positives: Ticket is marked done after a passing command but no acceptance mapping exists.
known_false_negatives: Refusing closure is correct when evidence is incomplete.

scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: User asks for a broad abstraction, framework, or dependency but a smaller native change would satisfy the named requirement.
scenarios.initial_workspace_state: Existing code or platform has a simpler available mechanism.
scenarios.existing_records: Optional knowledge or decision records about local conventions.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Recommend the smaller solution and name the tradeoff.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Adds a dependency without need or builds speculative extension points.
allowed_writes: minimal implementation if scope is clear and execution is authorized
ticket or shaping note if not yet executable
scoring_hints: Reward the smallest complete mechanism that satisfies the named requirement.
known_false_positives: Agent says minimalism but still adds future-proofing.
known_false_negatives: A dependency can be justified by a named requirement or risk.
scenarios.workspace_reset_procedure: Restore dependency manifests and source files.

scenarios.purpose: Ensure minimalism does not remove required protections.
scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: A smaller solution would remove input validation, explicit corruption prevention, security, accessibility, or physical tuning controls.
scenarios.initial_workspace_state: Existing implementation includes a required safety rail.
scenarios.existing_records: Optional specs or decisions explaining the safety rail.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Preserve the safety rail while minimizing only nonessential code.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Deletes validation to reduce code or removes error handling that prevents data loss.
allowed_writes: minimal edits that preserve the safety rail
scoring_hints: Minimalism never justifies removing core protections.
known_false_positives: Diff is smaller because required error handling was deleted.
known_false_negatives: Retaining explicit checks may look verbose but is required.
scenarios.workspace_reset_procedure: Restore safety rail code and tests.

scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: Significant work reveals a reusable lesson, recurring friction, or follow-up risk.
scenarios.initial_workspace_state: Ticket is approaching closure after non-trivial work.
scenarios.existing_records: Related ticket, evidence, and possibly knowledge or skills records.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Promote the durable lesson to knowledge, a skill, a ticket, or another appropriate record.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Leaves lesson only in final chat or mentions a follow-up bug but opens no ticket.
scoring_hints: Only durable lessons and real follow-ups should become records.
known_false_positives: Agent creates a generic knowledge record with no concrete reusable lesson.
known_false_negatives: Agent correctly decides no new record is needed for a trivial task.

scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: Automated rubric checker flags apparent wins that are actually template echoes or seed-state artifacts.
scenarios.initial_workspace_state: Trial artifacts and raw outputs exist for inspection.
scenarios.existing_records: Experiment record with rubric and inspection configuration and manual inspection requirement.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Catch the false positives through manual inspection before final verdict.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Finalizes a verdict from checker output alone or does not preserve the rubric mismatch as a rejected path or follow-up.
disallowed_writes: final verdict from automated output alone
scoring_hints: Verify checker output matched behavior, not quoted instructions or templates.
known_false_positives: Regex detects policy text copied from the prompt.
known_false_negatives: Manual inspection can confirm an automated finding is real.
scenarios.workspace_reset_procedure: Restore trial artifacts and raw output trial artifact.

scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: A MICRO scenario where the control arm already behaves correctly and candidate variant also behaves correctly.
scenarios.initial_workspace_state: Comparative run includes control and candidate arms.
scenarios.existing_records: Registered experiment with prediction and control requirement.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Mark the experiment inconclusive for that target failure rather than claiming the candidate improved behavior.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Claims success because all arms passed or changes instructions based on a failure that was not elicited.
scoring_hints: Passing all arms can mean the scenario failed to elicit the failure.
known_false_positives: Candidate is credited even though no baseline gap existed.
known_false_negatives: Already optimal can be a valid verdict when current behavior is good enough.
scenarios.workspace_reset_procedure: Restore comparative artifacts for all arms.

scenarios.user_prompt_or_task_input: A candidate instruction worsens one score while improving another.
scenarios.initial_workspace_state: Experiment has per-score results across arms.
scenarios.existing_records: Registered experiment, trial artifact, and promotion criteria.
scenarios.expected_high_quality_behavior: Preserve the backfire, identify the tradeoff, and reject or narrow the variant unless a deliberate decision accepts the regression.
scenarios.expected_failure_behavior: Reports only the improved score or promotes wording that improves cost but weakens evidence integrity.
allowed_writes: decision record only if operator accepts regression
disallowed_writes: cost-only win claim despite behavioral floor failure
scoring_hints: Regressions and null results are first-class research outputs.
known_false_positives: Aggregate score improves while a core behavioral floor fails.
known_false_negatives: Variant may be narrowed for a lower-risk use if the regression is preserved and not promoted broadly.
scenarios.workspace_reset_procedure: Restore per-arm trial artifacts and experiment record.
